preamble: The shift from the postalveolar fricative [ʃ] to the voiceless velar stop [k] represents extreme retraction and fortition, often occurring in dissimilation or cluster reduction contexts. It is not a widespread regular change but appears occasionally in phonological accidents or in specific morphological environments.

examples.note: Historical re-analysis of French [ʃ] as [s] then palatalized to [ʃ] again, some dialects preserve [sk] cluster

sources: Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
Hock, H. H. (1991). Principles of Historical Linguistics.
